LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOSELITO LAURORA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT RAM PARMAR'S RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, FOR AN ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS AND REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT PARMAR IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE WITHOUT OBJECTIONS RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF LAURORA'S AND PLAINTIFF MACAHILAS' FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL AND FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT. DEFENDANT PARMAR IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE A RESPONSE WITHOUT OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF LAURORA'S AND PLAINTIFF MACHAHILAS' SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 2. DEFENDANT PARMAR IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE A RESPONSE WITHOUT OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF LAURORA'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION NO. 31 AND PLAINTIFF MACAHILAS' REQUEST FOR ADMISSION NOS. 8 AND 33. ALL RESPONSES ARE TO BE PRODUCED WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-11-2014 Vacated

Plaintiff Joselito Laurora'S Motion To Compel Defendant Ram Parmar'S Responses To Discovery, For An Order Establishing Admissions And Requests For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Joselito Laurora's Motion To Compel Defendant Ram Parmar's Responses To Discovery, For An Order Establishing Admissions And Requests For Sanctions IS Granted. Defendant Parmar is ordered to produce without objections responses to Plaintiff Laurora's and Plaintiff Macahilas' Form Interrogatories - General and Form Interrogatories - Employment. Defendant Parmar is ordered to produce a response without objection to Plaintiff Laurora's and Plaintiff Machahilas' Special Interrogatory No. 2. Defendant Parmar is ordered to produce a response without objection to Plaintiff Laurora's Request for Admission No. 31 and Plaintiff Macahilas' Request for Admission Nos. 8 and 33. All responses are to be produced within 30 days of this order. No sanctions are awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Ntc Of Mtn Anf Mtn Compel Deft Ram Parmar'S Responses To Discovery, For An Order Establishing Admissions And Requests For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRISTEN ROSS OF BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, TEL. NO. 835-6777 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
RAM PARMAR, TEL. NO. (408) 239-6077, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - PLAINTIFF DID NOT STIPULATE FOR THE JUDGE PRO TEM TO HEAR THE MATTER. PLAINTIFF JOSELITO LAURORA's Motion To Compel Defendant Ram Parmar's Responses To Discovery, For An Order Establishing Admissions And Requests For Sanctions IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 10, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. Pro Tem Judge: Elizabeth Riles; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.

Mtn To Quash Deft'S Subpoenas
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Curiale Wilson, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, Tel. 415.835.6777, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFFs JOSELITO LAURORA and MERLYN MACAHILAS' Motion To Quash Defendant's Subpoenas is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The records to be produced pursuant to the subpoena to Kaiser shall be limited to records since 2011 concerning Plaintiff Macahila's Inpingement Syndrome; records during September of 2012 concerning her heavy bleeding and cancer diagnosis; and records since September 2011 concerning psychiatric or psychological information. The records to be produced pursuant to the subpoena to Catholic Charities CYO shall be limited to payroll and time records from September 2011 to the present and documents regarding Plaintiff's medical condition and any request for a leave of absence or time off from Catholic Charities. The parties shall meet and confer concerning a stipulated protective order. Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint alleges that Defendant Parmer failed to provide Plaintiff Macahilas sick/medical leave in or around September 2011 when Mrs. Macahilas was diagnosed with Impingement Syndrome (FAC Par. 34). It also alleges that Defendant Parmer fired Mrs. Macahilas when she requested that she be allowed to leaver her shift early on the morning of September 23, 2012 to seek medical attention at Kaiser as a result of heavy bleeding that was not subsiding (FAC Par. 38). Portions of the Kaiser medical records are directly relevant to Plaintiff's claims and essential to the fair resolution of the lawsuit. Britt v. Sup.Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 859. Defendants are entitled to discovery concerning psychiatric or psychological information in Plaintiff's medical records since September 2011. The sixth cause of action of Plaintiffs' complaint asserts a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress against all defendants. The case relied upon by Plaintiff, Tilo v. Sup.Ct. (1997) 55 Cal. App. 4th 1379-1382-83, does not control because the plaintiff in that case did not assert a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
